unmapped.position_conflict_disclosure: Paper book holds a long NVDA position entered at $205.19, current mark approximately $210.69, tagged robotics-humanoid. NVDA is a broad AI and robotics infrastructure expression, not a precise humanoid actuator or pilot thesis vehicle. No direct positions in TSLA, SYM, SERV, TER, or ABB. Live book gate not yet cleared; all positions are paper only.
unmapped.uncertainty: Primary catalyst (Figure AI financing or IPO) is private with no disclosed timeline. Scanner data is approximately 7 weeks stale; current IV and convergence scores may have moved materially. Corpus claims dated May 2026 with no updates since. Watchlist verdict reflects evidence strength, not catalyst imminence. Q2 2026 Figure window may already be closing.

original_issue.falsification: Figure AI postpones financing catalyst past Q2 2026 while BMW deployment remains a non-revenue pilot — watchlist conviction degrades, sector drops from primary candidates. Tesla removes Optimus production milestones from quarterly disclosure or shelves production beyond internal-use scope through 2027. BMW, Mercedes-Benz, or another top-ten auto OEM cancels a humanoid pilot citing unit economics, safety acceptance, or reliability as the primary reason.

title: The Robot Has Left the Lab; the Market Has Not
summary: Humanoid robotics has moved from demos into commercial pilots at BMW, Mercedes-Benz, Tesla, Symbotic, and Serve Robotics, but the options scanner returns one deep-OTM TSLA contract at MEDIUM convergence — the corpus is ahead of market pricing, the revaluation catalyst is Figure AI's financing or IPO timing, and that trigger has not moved.

claims.statement: Humanoid robotics has moved from demos into commercial pilots at BMW, Mercedes-Benz, Tesla, Symbotic, and Serve Robotics, but the options scanner returns one deep-OTM TSLA contract at MEDIUM convergence — the corpus is ahead of market pricing, the revaluation catalyst is Figure AI's financing or IPO timing, and that trigger has not moved.
claims.falsification: Figure AI postpones financing catalyst past Q2 2026 while BMW deployment remains a non-revenue pilot — watchlist conviction degrades, sector drops from primary candidates. Tesla removes Optimus production milestones from quarterly disclosure or shelves production beyond internal-use scope through 2027. Harmonic-drive, actuator, or rare-earth magnet suppliers disclose broad lead-time normalization while humanoid developers stop citing component cost or supply as a scaling constraint. BMW, Mercedes-Benz, or another top-ten auto OEM cancels a humanoid pilot citing unit economics, safety acceptance, or reliability as the primary reason.

render.body: The Robot Has Left the Lab; the Market Has Not

render.heading: Thesis
render.body: Humanoid robotics has moved from demos into commercial pilots at BMW, Mercedes-Benz, Tesla, Symbotic, and Serve Robotics, but the options scanner returns one deep-OTM TSLA contract at MEDIUM convergence — the corpus is ahead of market pricing, the revaluation catalyst is Figure AI's financing or IPO timing, and that trigger has not moved.

render.heading: Signals
render.body:
Commercial pilots as revenue-inflection leading indicator: Tesla, BMW, Mercedes-Benz, Symbotic, and Serve Robotics have live or announced commercial pilots. Pre-revenue but OEM conversion to production contracts is the next observable event. Sourced from named company IR filings. (strength: 0.8)

Figure AI financing or IPO as public-comps rerating event: Figure's private mark anchors humanoid optionality pricing for Teradyne, ABB, Symbotic, and Serve comparables. Q2 2026 IPO window narrowing without announcement as of publication date. (strength: 0.82)

Harmonic-drive and rare-earth magnet supply as production volume bottleneck: Strain-wave gearing and neodymium magnets are the binding mechanical constraint on scaling humanoid production volume and controlling unit economics. Evidenced in harmonic-drive supplier data, DOE neodymium reporting, and Teradyne earnings disclosures. (strength: 0.78)

Scanner returning one deep-OTM TSLA contract; MEDIUM convergence across sector: April 28 sweep: 13 contracts across TSLA, ISRG, SYM, SERV — one passing. TSLA $455 call, 20.3% OTM, delta 0.08, IV 44.4%, asymmetry 6.13, MEDIUM convergence 0.50. NVDA HIGH convergence 0.981 but broad AI expression. No passing contracts for SYM, SERV, TER, or ABB. Scanner data is approximately 7 weeks stale. (strength: 0.85)

render.heading: What would falsify it
render.body: Figure AI postpones financing catalyst past Q2 2026 while BMW deployment remains a non-revenue pilot — watchlist conviction degrades, sector drops from primary candidates. Tesla removes Optimus production milestones from quarterly disclosure or shelves production beyond internal-use scope through 2027. Harmonic-drive, actuator, or rare-earth magnet suppliers disclose broad lead-time normalization while humanoid developers stop citing component cost or supply as a scaling constraint. BMW, Mercedes-Benz, or another top-ten auto OEM cancels a humanoid pilot citing unit economics, safety acceptance, or reliability as the primary reason.
